meta.title: The Protestant Canon DEFENDED
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund (with Ben and Javier Purdomo)

questions.question: According to the discussion, how many books does the Ethiopian Orthodox canon reportedly reach?
What distinction do the speakers emphasize between infallibility and authority?
options: Infallibility is metaphysical (cannot err); authority is practical (binding)
They are identical in church teaching
questions.answer: Infallibility is metaphysical (cannot err); authority is practical (binding)

questions.question: Which historical figure is cited as having been excommunicated and later canonized, illustrating fallible church discipline?
Roughly how many books does the Roman Catholic canon include in this video’s summary?
What parallel question do the guests suggest asking Roman Catholics who press the infallible canon?
options: Can you show an infallible list of infallible traditions?
Where is the Bible in the original autographs?
What is the date of the Second Coming?
questions.answer: Can you show an infallible list of infallible traditions?

questions.question: Which council’s decree on Scripture is said to be read in two ways—exclusionary (canon closed) vs. inclusionary (baseline, possibly more books later)?
Who is named as associated with the exclusionary reading of Trent’s canon list?
Which Eastern Orthodox jurisdiction is said to publicly align its canon with the Roman Catholic list on its website?
What philosophical problem does Gavin raise against requiring infallibility at every step of recognizing infallible teaching?
Which church father is central to the discussion of a two-tier Old Testament (canonical vs. ecclesiastical/deuterocanonical)?
What does the New Catholic Encyclopedia material cited say Jerome judged about ecclesiastical books?
options: They were circulated for good spiritual reading but not recognized as authoritative Scripture
They are equal to the Gospels
questions.answer: They were circulated for good spiritual reading but not recognized as authoritative Scripture

questions.question: Which scholar’s work is cited as quoting Augustine verbatim in a section of the Gelasian material after the Council of Rome—undermining that section’s historical value?
Which Catholic author who wrote on why Catholic Bibles are bigger is said to avoid apologetic reliance on the Council of Rome?
What is the misuse alleged regarding Jerome’s line about submitting to the judgment of the churches (Against Rufinus)?
options: It concerns two Greek versions of Daniel, not deuterocanon vs. Masoretic
He was rejecting the New Testament
He was endorsing the Muratorian Fragment only
questions.answer: It concerns two Greek versions of Daniel, not deuterocanon vs. Masoretic

questions.question: Which father is said to cite Wisdom and Sirach in Against the Arians yet exclude those books in the Festal Letter of 367?
Which Lutheran theologians are named as citing deuterocanonical books as Scripture while denying full canonicity?
options: Martin Luther and Philip Melanchthon only
John Calvin and Theodore Beza only

questions.question: Josephus’s Against Apion 1.8 is cited for what kind of Old Testament structure?
options: A single scroll of Torah only
A tripartite canon of 22 books closed by the time of Artaxerxes
questions.answer: A tripartite canon of 22 books closed by the time of Artaxerxes

questions.question: Melito of Sardis’s list is introduced through which church historian?
Roger Beckwith is cited regarding Melito’s unusual numbering—approximately how many books?
Gregory the Great is quoted as allowing testimony from books that are not canonical yet brought out for what purpose?
The Glossa Ordinaria is placed at latest in what period relative to Luther’s 95 Theses?
options: High Middle Ages, before the Reformation
questions.answer: High Middle Ages, before the Reformation

questions.question: What century is suggested as a watershed when some Protestants drift from nuanced historic views on deuterocanonical books?
Cyril of Jerusalem warns catechumens about disputed writings and points to how many Old Testament books from the seventy translators?
What image does Gavin use for how the debate first feels emotionally—Protestants vs. others?
What is given as the simplest positive reason for the Protestant canon at the end of the historical section?
options: Following the canon Jesus and the apostles used
questions.answer: Following the canon Jesus and the apostles used
